title: Future Undergraduate Students - Financial Aid Overview
content: At Queen's University, a university education is recognized as a major financial undertaking. While all Queen's students have the ability to excel academically, some may not be able to fully finance their education. Queen's University provides financial support throughout undergraduate studies.

title: Future Undergraduate Students - Ineligible Programs
content: Students entering the Faculty of Law, School of Medicine, Consecutive Education, or other graduate programs are not eligible for the awards listed in this section.

title: Financial Aid - Graduate and Professional Students
content: Graduate, Professional, Law and Medicine students should review financial aid options available specifically to them.

title: Financial Aid - Specific Student Groups
content: Specific financial aid options are available for part-time students, consecutive education students, student athletes, Queen's alumnae, mature women students, Indigenous students, Black and racialized students, and students with disabilities.

title: Financial Aid - International Students
content: International students should review additional financial aid options available specifically to them.

title: Future Students - Financial Aid Options
content: Future Queen’s students may access financial aid options available to current students as well as options that do not require an application.

title: Financial Aid & Awards Profile
content: Through the Financial Aid & Awards Profile, students are considered for Admission Bursaries, Major Access Awards, Commitment Bursary, Donor-funded awards and bursaries, Youth in Care, and the Promise Scholar program.

title: Major Admission Awards
content: Major Admission Awards are for students demonstrating superior academic ability, creative and original thinking, involvement in school or community activities, leadership qualities, and financial need. These awards are accessed through a single major admission award application.

title: Awards Requiring Individual Applications
content: Some awards are based on multiple criteria including academic achievement and require application through a single admission awards application.

title: Admission Awards - Eligibility Overview
content: Some admission awards may have additional eligibility requirements. Students should review the specific details of each award program to determine eligibility.

title: Admission Awards - First-Year Eligibility
content: To be eligible for most admission awards, students must be entering the first year of post-secondary education for the first time in a full-time Queen's undergraduate degree program on Queen's campus or at Bader College.

title: Admission Awards - CEGEP Transfer Students
content: Graduates from a 2-year CEGEP program transferring into their first year of a Queen's program remain eligible for grades-based admission scholarships.

title: Admission Awards - Prior Post-Secondary Education
content: Students with prior post-secondary education are not eligible for Queen's admission awards.

title: Admission Awards - Domestic and International Students
content: Domestic students and international students, including U.S. students, are considered for automatic grade-based admission scholarships.

title: Admission Awards - International and U.S. Students
content: International and U.S. students should review the International and U.S. Students section for additional admission awards.

title: Admission Awards - Awards Average Calculation
content: Queen's calculates an awards average using grades available at the time an offer of admission is made.

title: Admission Awards - Final Grade Review
content: Final grades are assessed for scholarship and award consideration for students who accept their offer of admission to Queen's.

title: Admission Awards - Post-Admission Review
content: If a scholarship is not offered at the time of admission, final grades will be reviewed after receipt of final grade reports from the Ontario Universities' Application Centre in July or August.

title: Admission Awards - Summer Courses
content: Summer courses taken immediately prior to the admit term will not be used in the assessment of the final grade average.

title: Admission Awards - Full-Time Requirement
content: To receive and retain an admission scholarship or award, students must be admitted and registered as a full-time student during the fall and winter academic year.

title: Renewable Academic Awards - Enrollment Requirement
content: To remain eligible for renewal of a renewable academic-based award, students must remain registered in an 80% course load, typically 24 units during fall and winter.

title: Renewable Academic Awards - GPA Requirement
content: Students must achieve a minimum GPA of 3.5 to remain eligible for renewal of a renewable academic-based award.

title: Renewable Awards - Policy Note
content: Not all academic-based awards are renewable. Students should review award terms on SOLUS or award descriptions to confirm renewability and refer to the Queen’s Renewable Awards Policy for further details.

title: Major Admission Awards - Academic Guideline Note
content: The academic guideline listed for major admission awards is not based on the awards average calculation method.

text: Queen's University First-Generation Student Admission Pathway promotes diversity, inclusion, and equitable opportunities, enhancing student belonging and academic excellence. Supports are available from application through the summer before first year.

First-generation supports include: general application support, academic advising, education and career assistance, financial planning (OSAP, scholarships, bursaries), parental assurance, and first-year transition supports.

Eligibility: A first-generation student is the first in their family (excluding siblings) to pursue post-secondary education. Eligible students include those whose parents did not attend post-secondary education, even if they have siblings who have, or students affiliated with community partners. Admission is based on academic preparedness and potential.

Students admitted through this pathway are encouraged to use services at the Student Experience Office, Student Awards Office, and all academic advising and support services available to Queen's students.

Workshops and Resource Guides: Queen's offers over 20 workshops including Discover Queen's, Navigating OUAC & Post-Secondary Applications, OSAP & Financing Education. Resource guides are available via email at 1gen@queensu.ca for those unable to attend workshops.

Partnership Opportunities: Community-based agencies and high schools serving equity-deserving youth can partner with Queen's to support students navigating higher education. Campus trips can be arranged to familiarize students with Queen's campus and partners. Contact accessinclusion@queensu.ca for partnerships or trips.

Student Testimonial: Wei Hu, Smith School of Business ’24, shared that using the First-Generation Pathway was helpful throughout the application process, providing mentorship and confidence for first-year success.
Equity Ambassadors: Students interested in undergraduate experiences as first-generation or equity-deserving students can connect with the Equity Ambassadors for peer guidance and support.
